title: Make `zed-agentops-init.sh` resolve workflow rules relative to the script location
summary: Fix the bootstrap bug where `.rules` generation depends on the caller's current working directory instead of the packaged script location. This ticket updates `zed-agentops-init.sh` to resolve workflow rule assets from `BASH_SOURCE[0]` so installed usage and source-tree usage both write `.rules` reliably.
outputs: Reliable `.rules` creation when `zed-agentops-init` is run from arbitrary working directories
Regression tests covering installed-style invocation and script-relative asset discovery
acceptance_criteria: The init script no longer uses `${PWD}` as the primary lookup base for workflow rule assets.
The init script computes a script-relative base path from `BASH_SOURCE[0]` and uses it to resolve packaged rule resources.
Running installed `zed-agentops-init <project>` from a working directory unrelated to the repository still writes `<project>/.rules`.
Running `src/agentops_mcp_server/zed-agentops-init.sh <project>` from the source tree still writes `<project>/.rules` successfully.
Existing `.rules` overwrite and backup semantics remain unchanged except for the path resolution fix.
Tests cover the regression scenario where installed execution previously failed because rule assets were searched relative to the caller's current directory.
tasks: Add script self-location logic near the top of `zed-agentops-init.sh` using `BASH_SOURCE[0]`.
Replace `${PWD}`-based workflow rules source paths with script-relative packaged resource paths.
Preserve compatibility for source-tree execution while prioritizing packaged adjacent assets.
Keep the current target `.rules` skip and backup behavior unchanged.
Add or update tests to assert script-relative lookup behavior and `.rules` creation under installed-style invocation.
notes: This ticket implements the chosen 0.5.1 approach of fixing bootstrap path resolution inside the shell script rather than introducing a new environment-variable contract.
The main risk is incorrect path normalization on packaged installs, so tests should exercise invocation from outside the repository root.
The fallback rules text is already packaged and should remain a valid source for `.rules` generation even when repository-only paths do not exist.
